message:
refactor(stream): migrate all stream combinators from manual Unpin to pin-project

Replace hand-written Unpin impls and Pin::new(&mut self.stream) patterns
across nine stream combinators with pin_project derive macros and
projected field access. This removes the S: Unpin bound from all
combinator Stream impls, allowing them to compose with !Unpin streams
(e.g. async generators, recursive stream types).

Affected combinators: Count, Debounce, Enumerate, ForEach,
ForEachAsync, Fuse, Peekable, Skip, SkipWhile, Throttle, Zip.

Key changes per combinator:
- Count: Add #[pin] stream, remove manual Unpin impl, project in poll()
- Debounce: Add #[pin] stream, remove S::Item: Unpin bounds on all impl
  blocks, project in poll_next(), dereference projected scalar fields
- Enumerate: Add #[pin] stream, project in poll_next()
- ForEach: Add #[pin] stream, remove manual Unpin impl, project in poll()
- ForEachAsync: Add #[pin] stream and #[pin] pending, remove manual
  Unpin impl, remove Fut: Unpin bound, use as_pin_mut() and .set() for
  the Option<Fut> projection
- Fuse: Add #[pin] stream (Option<S>), use as_pin_mut() for
  Option projection in poll_next()
- Peekable: Change poll_peek(&mut self) to poll_peek(self: Pin<&mut Self>),
  project internally, update all call sites to Pin::new(&mut stream).poll_peek()
- Skip/SkipWhile: Add #[pin] stream, remove F: Unpin bound on SkipWhile,
  project in poll_next()
- Throttle: Add #[pin] stream, project in poll_next(), dereference
  projected Duration/Option<Instant>
- Zip: Add #[pin] stream1/stream2, remove manual Unpin impl, remove
  S1/S2: Unpin bounds, project in poll_next(), add size_hint() that
  accounts for queued items, add test for buffered-item size_hint accuracy

Also updates stream_sink_integration test to use Pin::new(&mut stream)
for poll_peek calls to match the new signature.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
